root_text.sn56.49:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso sinerussa pabbatarājassa satta muggamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhipeyya.
root_text.sn56.49:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yā vā satta muggamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā, yo vā sinerupabbatarājā”ti?
root_text.sn56.49:1.6: Saṅkhampi na upenti, upanidhampi na upenti, kalabhāgampi na upenti sinerupabbatarājānaṁ upanidhāya satta muggamattiyo pāsāṇasakkharā upanikkhittā”ti.
root_text.sn56.49:1.7: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa diṭṭhisampannassa puggalassa abhisametāvino etadeva bahutaraṁ dukkhaṁ yadidaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ; appamattakaṁ avasiṭṭhaṁ.
root_text.sn56.49:1.8: Saṅkhampi na upeti, upanidhampi na upeti, kalabhāgampi na upeti, purimaṁ dukkhakkhandhaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ pariyādinnaṁ upanidhāya yadidaṁ sattakkhattuparamatā;
root_text.sn56.49:1.9: yo ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.sn56.49:2.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.49:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose a person was to place on Sineru, the king of mountains, seven pebbles the size of mung beans.
translation_text.sn56.49:1.3: Which is more: the seven pebbles the size of mung beans? Or Sineru, the king of mountains?”
translation_text.sn56.49:1.4: “Sir, Sineru, the king of mountains, is certainly more.
translation_text.sn56.49:1.5: The seven pebbles the size of mung beans are tiny.
translation_text.sn56.49:1.6: Compared to Sineru, they don’t count, there’s no comparison, they’re not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.49:1.7: “In the same way, for an individual with comprehension, a noble disciple accomplished in view, the suffering that’s over and done with is more, what’s left is tiny.
translation_text.sn56.49:1.8: Compared to the mass of suffering in the past that’s over and done with, it doesn’t count, there’s no comparison, it’s not worth a fraction, since there are at most seven more lives.
translation_text.sn56.49:1.9: Such an individual truly understands about suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path.
translation_text.sn56.49:2.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.49:1.3: vā → yo ca (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr) | sinerupabbatarājā → sineru vā pabbatarājā (bj)
